title: Hungry and tired
pedagogy: Vocabulary: หิว (high ห sounded + live ว-glide → rising), เหนื่อย (ห-nam น: high + mai ek → low). Contrast: sounded ห (หิว) vs silent leading ห (เหนื่อย) — same letter, two jobs. หิวข้าว compound reviews ข้าว; มาก intensifier recombined.

exercises.title: Hunger says what it's hungry for
body: Thai hunger is specific: หิวข้าว hungry (for food), หิวน้ำ thirsty (hungry for water). The compound tells you the craving.
Both new words start with ห — but differently. In หิว the ห is the sound itself (h-). In เหนื่อย it's silent, only there to bend the tone of น.
One letter, two jobs. Your Reading Thai training pays off exactly here.

exercises.explanation: หิวข้าว — the everyday “I'm hungry”. Swap in น้ำ and you've said you're thirsty; that chip wasn't wrong Thai, just a different complaint.
เหนื่อยมากค่ะ — feeling + มาก + particle. The all-purpose end-of-day sentence.
